title: Caesalpinia platycarpa
taxonomy: Caesalpinia platycarpa belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and fruit that resembles a pod. The genus Caesalpinia is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Fabaceae family, Caesalpinia platycarpa is classified as part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. The subfamily Caesalpinioideae is further divided into several tribes, including the Caesalpinieae tribe.

intro: Caesalpinia platycarpa is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the Caesalpinia tree. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections.
